sections.title: Q14: How do you evaluate the performance of a computer vision model?
sections.content:
**Score:** 92/100

**Answer:**

**Classification Metrics:**
- **Accuracy:** (TP + TN) / Total
- **Precision:** TP / (TP + FP)
- **Recall/Sensitivity:** TP / (TP + FN)
- **F1-Score:** Harmonic mean of precision and recall
- **ROC-AUC:** Area under ROC curve

**Object Detection Metrics:**
- **IoU (Intersection over Union):** Overlap between predicted and ground truth boxes
- **mAP (Mean Average Precision):** Average precision across all classes
- **AP (Average Precision):** Area under precision-recall curve per class

**Segmentation Metrics:**
- **IoU/Jaccard Index:** Intersection / Union
- **Dice Coefficient:** 2 × |A ∩ B| / (|A| + |B|)
- **Pixel Accuracy:** Correctly classified pixels / Total pixels

**Best Practices:**
- Use multiple metrics for comprehensive evaluation
- Consider class imbalance (use F1, mAP over accuracy)
- Evaluate on diverse test sets (different conditions)
- Monitor inference time and resource usage
- Cross-validation for robust estimates

---

---

**Difficulty:** ⭐⭐⭐
**Category:** CV
